title: Cards or list view
question: Should the explorer show decisions only as cards, or also offer a plain list view?
rationale: Offer two views via a segmented control: the designed card grid and a compact one-row-per-decision list, both keeping the same stage grouping and pop-up, with your pick remembered. Cards show a preview; the list is denser and quicker to scan when there are many — so offering both beats forcing one.

history.from: A single toggle icon in the top bar that flipped cards ⇄ list
history.reason: Replaced with a two-option icon segmented control (grid / list) inside the unified toolbar cluster, so the active view is explicit rather than inferred from one toggling glyph

options.label: Toggle between cards and a list (remembered)

tradeoffs.note: pick whichever reads better; same grouping and pop-up either way
the list packs more decisions on screen
cards stay the default, designed view
a second row renderer plus a remembered toggle
cards take more room; fewer fit on screen
loses the card grid the redesign was chosen for
